title: Reverse Engineering
html:
The skill set Yahir picked up in the spring-2025 CS security capstone — disassembling BusyBox in IDA Pro inside REMnux, reading the libwebp Huffman heap overflow CVE writeup the same week, then the RL coursework. A distinct narrative thread from Yahir's product-builder identity — pure analysis, low-creative, high-puzzle. Adjacent to malware analysis lab (dynamic side) and the mobile malware thread.

TL;DR

  • 20 inbound citations, almost entirely from a tight May 14 → May 20 2025 window plus the May 19 IDA marathon.
  • Tooling: IDA Pro (disassembler), BusyBox (target binary), REMnux (Linux analysis distro), pseudocode export.
  • Paired with buffer overflow / WebP CVE coursework reading the same week.
  • Functions in the corpus as the cognitive sister discipline to product-building — the take-it-apart skill that mirrors Yahir's instinct to disassemble market positioning (aeo agency competitive-research moves use the same energy).
  • Continues into malware analysis lab dynamic work in June 2025.

Key facts

  • May 14 — first WebP CVE reading. 2025 05 14 screenshot 2025 05 14 at 9 46 10 am 97029c8a / 2025 05 14 screenshot 2025 05 14 at 9 46 32 am ef6b2625 — Yahir reading the libwebp vulnerability writeup as warm-up.
  • May 19 — IDA Pro marathon. 10+ screenshots from a single afternoon (2025 05 19 screenshot 2025 05 19 at 3 18 52 pm a7016caa → 2025 05 19 screenshot 2025 05 19 at 4 20 29 pm 3a4a8569). Disassembly + cross-reference work.
  • May 20 — pseudocode + final. Multiple captures across the day; final pseudocode at 2025 05 20 screenshot 2025 05 20 at 3 27 50 pm 9f0f6022.
  • Carry-over to dynamic analysis. June 2025 cluster (see malware analysis lab) is the natural follow-on — once you've read the binary, you watch it run.
  • Applied to non-security. The take-it-apart instinct shows up in aeo agency competitive research and the BroMenu data model reverse-thinking.

  • 2025 05 14 screenshot 2025 05 14 at 9 46 10 am 97029c8a — WebP CVE reading

Plus ~6 additional May 19/20 cluster screenshots.

Connections

  • ida pro · busybox · remnux — primary tooling
  • webp cve · buffer overflow — adjacent CVE coursework
  • malware analysis lab · network forensics · wireshark — dynamic-analysis siblings
  • cs coursework · osu — institutional context
  • reinforcement learning · monte carlo method — same-semester ML work
  • mobile malware · dvwa — broader security domain
